channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Almost Half of House Democrats Vote to End Aid to Israel
item.description: The measure failed, but the level of support among Democrats exposed a stark shift in the party away from backing the Jewish state.
item.dc:creator: Annie Karni
item.media:description: Representative Hakeem Jeffries, the Democratic minority leader and a staunch supporter of Israel, hosted two caucus meetings to discuss the amendment, a highly unusual move that indicated how fraught the politics around Israel have become for Democrats.

item.title: Iranian American Woman Held in Iran on Spying Charges Is Released
item.description: The dual Iranian-U.S. citizen, Dena Karari, had her passport seized in December 2024 and had not been able to leave Iran as she faced espionage charges.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: U.S. Military Again Blockades Iranian Ports After Collapse of Cease-Fire
item.description: President Trump ordered U.S. warships and aircraft to stop vessels going to and from Iran. Enforcing a blockade takes a huge commitment of warships and aircraft.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The U.S.S. Abraham Lincoln in San Diego in 2022. According to the U.S. Naval Institute, as of Monday it is anchoring one of two aircraft carrier strike groups deployed in the Northern Arabian Sea.

item.title: Todd Blanche, Trump’s Attorney General Pick, Faces Crucial Hurdle After Rocky Hearing
item.description: Even a single Republican “no” vote would block Mr. Blanche’s nomination from consideration by the full Senate, which could sink his confirmation.
item.dc:creator: Glenn Thrush, Devlin Barrett, Michael Gold and Alan Feuer
item.media:description: Todd Blanche on Wednesday at his confirmation hearing to be attorney general. It is not clear when the Senate Judiciary Committee will schedule a vote.

item.title: Hegseth Plans to Screen All Troops, Including Women, for Low Testosterone
item.description: Pete Hegseth, as defense secretary, has sought to cultivate an image as a manosphere-friendly leader.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe and Azeen Ghorayshi
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: The goal, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said in a social media message, was a “High-T Department of War.”

item.title: Trump Administration to Impose New Tariffs on Brazil
item.description: The United States accused the country of unfair trade practices. The tariff will replace ones that the Supreme Court struck down.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson and Ana Ionova
item.media:description: President Trump first imposed tariffs on Brazil last year in an effort to help former President Jair Bolsonaro avoid a jail sentence.

item.title: Wildfire Smoke Spreads Across the Northeast as Temperatures Spike
item.description: A heat dome is responsible for miserably high temperatures, and it’s also helping to push smoke from wildfires to the east.
item.dc:creator: Judson Jones and Mark Walker

item.title: Flash Flood Emergency Is Issued in Texas After Days of Pounding Rain
item.description: Roads became rivers on Wednesday as emergency officials in towns around San Antonio raced to rescue people who were stranded.
item.dc:creator: Judson Jones and Christine Hauser

item.title: Video Pushed Local Police to Come Clean. D.H.S. Is Ignoring the Lessons.
item.description: The agency has said little about two fatal encounters in the past week. Local police departments have learned to be forthcoming.
item.dc:creator: Shaila Dewan and Esmé E. Deprez
item.media:credit: Meridith Kohut for The New York Times
item.media:description: A makeshift memorial for Lorenzo Salgado Araujo near the site where he was killed in Houston.

item.title: F.B.I. Searches Houston ICE Shooting Van for Drugs
item.description: Federal investigators believe illegal drugs were inside the vehicle, though the immigration agents involved in the encounter do not appear to have known that.
item.dc:creator: J. David Goodman
item.media:credit: Meridith Kohut for The New York Times
item.media:description: Family members embraced next to a makeshift memorial for Lorenzo Salgado Araujo at the location where he was shot by an I.C.E. agent in Houston.

item.title: Trump Says He’ll Seek to Replace Immigrant Truck Drivers With Veterans
item.description: The president has pushed to curtail the commercial licenses of immigrants, including legal residents, whom he has blamed for crashes.
item.dc:creator: Aishvarya Kavi
item.media:description: President Trump mentioned a plan to crack down on truck drivers in the United States without legal status and to replace them with veterans during a panel at a military investment summit in Carlisle, Pa., on Wednesday.

item.title: India Is Moving Fast to Build A.I. Data Centers. A Coastal City May Pay the Price.
item.description: With India lagging in the technology, officials are embracing giant data centers. But critics say the megaprojects will use up energy and water, without providing long-term jobs.
item.dc:creator: Mujib Mashal and Hari Kumar
item.media:credit: Priyadarshini Ravichandran for The New York Times
item.media:description: The port city of Visakhapatnam in the state of Andhra Pradesh is rapidly transforming into India’s coastal gateway for global tech infrastructure, fueled by massive A.I. data centers.

item.title: Europe Wants to Break Free From American and Chinese Technology. But How?
item.description: France and Germany want to quit relying on America and China for key technology like artificial intelligence, but they’re having to choose where to do it.
item.dc:creator: Jim Tankersley and Mark Landler
item.media:description: Sébastien Lecornu, the French prime minister, has called for France to increase its independence from American technology firms, announcing plans to replace Zoom with a French-developed version.

item.title: Australia to Impose Energy and Water Guardrails on Data Centers Amid A.I. Boom
item.description: The country will also seek to protect the rights of creators of work used to train artificial intelligence models, as it aims to impose parameters on the growing industry.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Kim and Laura Chung
item.media:description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese announced the new data center requirements at the University of Sydney on Wednesday.

item.title: New York Times Files Motion to Quash Subpoenas of Its Journalists
item.description: The Justice Department is seeking to compel grand jury testimony as part of an investigation into Times reporting on the president’s new Air Force One jet.
item.dc:creator: Michael M. Grynbaum
item.media:description: President Trump boarding the new Air Force One at Joint Base Andrews in Maryland this month.

item.title: Our Top Editor on Subpoenas of Times Journalists
item.description: The Trump administration issued subpoenas to reporters who wrote about security concerns involving the new Air Force One. Joe Kahn, the executive editor of The New York Times, responds to what he calls a “naked attempt to intimidate” the newspaper.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Kahn, Jillian Eugenios, Megan DiTrolio, Santiago García Muñoz, Alejandro Soto Goico, Zach Caldwell, Luke Piotrowski and Mike Abrams
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: Investigators Seek Information From Government Officials as Part of Air Force One Leak Investigation
item.description: After The New York Times reported on security concerns related to the Qatari-donated jet, the F.B.I. sought to speak with several people who flew aboard the plane with President Trump last week and asked for their phones.
item.dc:creator: Maggie Haberman and Glenn Thrush
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: President Trump on July 1 at Joint Base Andrews next to the jet donated by Qatar, before its first flight as Air Force One.

item.title: ‘The Odyssey’ Review: Passion in Every Frame
item.description: Few directors close the divide between the art film and the blockbuster like Christopher Nolan.
item.dc:creator: Manohla Dargis

item.title: Archaeologists Unearth a 2,400-Year-Old Sanctuary for the Worship of Odysseus
item.description: The ruins of a 2,400-year-old complex suggest that, to the Greeks of antiquity, Homer’s protagonist was not just a character in a poem but a figure of genuine devotion.
item.dc:creator: Franz Lidz
item.media:credit: Fine Art Images/Heritage Images, via Getty Images
item.media:description: A 19th-century painting by the Swiss painter Arnold Böcklin depicting Odysseus fleeing the Cyclops Polyphemus.

item.title: Cyclospora Outbreak: What to Know About Symptoms and Food Safety
item.description: The parasite has sickened thousands across the United States. Experts have tips on how to stay safe.
item.dc:creator: Alice Callahan and Caroline Hopkins Legaspi
item.media:credit: Claire Merchlinsky/The New York Times; Photographs via Getty

item.title: Inside Maine Democrats’ Search for a New Senate Nominee After Graham Platner’s Exit
item.description: Democratic candidates are hastily building bare-bones campaigns and forgoing traditional outreach like TV ads in a sprint to appeal to party delegates, rather than Maine voters.
item.dc:creator: Bayliss Wagner
item.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times
item.media:description: Several Maine Democrats are jockeying to become the party’s new Senate nominee after Graham Platner, who won the midterm primary, dropped out last week.

item.title: Here Are the Maine Democrats Running to Replace Graham Platner
item.description: To win, they’ll have to convince Democrats at a July 25 convention that they’re the best candidate to take on Senator Susan Collins, a Republican.
item.dc:creator: Bayliss Wagner

item.title: G.O.P. Appears to Boost Socialist’s Primary Campaign for Wisconsin Governor
item.description: Republicans seem to view Francesca Hong as their weakest opponent in a general election. They are spending $2.2 million in an apparent attempt to aid her primary campaign.
item.dc:creator: Kellen Browning
item.media:credit: Jenn Ackerman for The New York Times
item.media:description: Some Democrats have fretted that state Representative Francesca Hong, a democratic socialist with unabashedly left-wing views, might win her primary and then deliver the governor’s mansion to Republicans.

item.title: Boat That Sank Near Alcatraz Rolled After Wave Struck, Officials Say
item.description: Rescuers described survivors trying to stay afloat in the San Francisco Bay. A brother of the boat’s owner died, and the authorities say they will end the search for the missing.
item.dc:creator: Soumya Karlamangla, Jacey Fortin and Madaleine Rubin
item.media:description: A San Francisco Fire Department boat searching near Alcatraz Island for three missing people after a recreational boat, the Volare, sank on Tuesday.

item.title: Meet Earth’s Newest Monkey. (And Check Out Its Orange Lips.)
item.description: It is only the fifth new African monkey identified as a new species in the past 75 years.
item.dc:creator: Kate Golembiewski

item.title: An American Strike Killed Iranian Civilians. Where’s the Accountability?
item.description: The Trump administration has failed to publicly address the mistaken missile strike that killed children during the Iran war.
item.dc:creator: W.J. Hennigan
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: England Lost in the World Cup. They Were Always Going to Lose.
item.description: The football team, like Britain itself, is burdened by the dead weight of impossible expectations.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Rose

item.title: Jimmy Fallon Pokes Fun at Joe Biden’s New Memoir
item.description: “It’s the second book Biden has written,” Fallon said of the former president. “The first was ‘The Odyssey.’”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)
The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)

item.title: Superagent? Obviously. Secret Weapon in 2028? Maybe.
item.description: Ari Emanuel’s empire has grown beyond Hollywood to include the U.F.C., WWE and some key figures in the manosphere. That could work out well for his brother Rahm Emanuel.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Zucker
item.media:credit: Brennan Smart for The New York Times
item.media:description: Ari Emanuel, a longtime Hollywood agent, is now closely connected to influential podcasters and sports figures who could wield power in 2028.
